meaning: n.中心,中央,向心性,集中性
examples: Google's PageRank is a variant of the Eigenvector centrality measure.
Yergin starts by reminding us of energy’s centrality to the modern age.
The neglect of freedom's centrality to liberal internationalism has consequences.
But with the advent of automobiles, the centrality of horses diminished and Hermes faced a crossroads.
Travis, understanding the historically unprecedented centrality he'd suddenly been granted, rose to the occasion with strained seriousness.
